questions.q: Which hook should you use to avoid recreating an object reference on every render?
questions.explanation: useMemo memoizes the computed value itself, recomputing only when dependencies change. useCallback memoizes a function reference. useRef persists a mutable value without triggering re-renders. useState causes a re-render whenever the value changes.

questions.q: When does React call the cleanup function returned by useEffect?
options: Before running the effect again and when the component unmounts
questions.explanation: React runs the cleanup before applying the next effect (if dependencies changed) and when the component unmounts. This ensures stale event listeners, subscriptions, or timers are removed before a new effect instance starts.

questions.q: What is the key purpose of React's reconciliation algorithm?
options: Efficiently computing the minimal set of DOM changes needed
questions.explanation: Reconciliation compares the new virtual DOM tree with the previous one (diffing) and computes the minimal set of actual DOM operations needed. It uses heuristics: same-type elements update in place, different-type elements destroy and recreate, and the key prop helps match list items across renders.
